commit.message:
feat: 实现 Collapsed Arena 核心系统 (Checkpoint 1-4)

使用 TDD 方法实现游戏基础设施和渲染层，所有测试通过 (55/55)

## 实现内容

### Checkpoint 1: 基础设施
- Vite + Vitest 构建系统
- Three.js 场景初始化
- 60fps 游戏循环
- Ammo.js WASM 加载

### Checkpoint 2: 配置系统
- 游戏配置外部化 (game-config.json)
- TypeScript 类型安全配置加载
- 所有数值来自设计文档 Section 9

### Checkpoint 3: 渲染层
- SceneManager: 场景和渲染器管理
- CameraController: 第三人称跟随摄像机
- LightingManager: 光照和阴影系统
- MaterialManager: 材质缓存和复用

### Checkpoint 4: 物理层
- PhysicsWorld: 物理世界模拟
- PhysicsBody: 刚体包装器
- 重力、碰撞检测

## 技术栈
- TypeScript 5.6
- Three.js 0.170
- Ammo.js 0.10
- Vite 6.0
- Vitest 2.0

## 测试
- 55 个测试全部通过
- 100% 代码覆盖率目标

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix lock contention: skip AuthProvider init on callback page

The AuthProvider's getSession() and the callback's exchangeCodeForSession()
compete for the same Supabase auth lock, causing 5s timeouts. Skip auth
init when on /auth/callback since the callback handles session setup.
Also remove diagnostic logging now that the anon key issue is fixed.

commit.message:
fix: clear stale forceRadiation before substep loop, re-add quadrupole drag display

The reconstruction at end-of-frame writes Larmor+quadrupole into
forceRadiation. On the next frame's first substep, the snapshot
captured this stale value before resetForces() ran. For neutral
particles (Larmor skipped), the drag accumulated frame over frame.

Fix: zero forceRadiation for all particles before the substep loop
so reconstructed values don't leak. Quadrupole drag display is
re-added now that the accumulation bug is fixed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add repeatable counters with settings cog UI

Counters can now cycle back to 1 after reaching a configurable max.
Settings cog replaces delete button, opening an inline view with a
repeat toggle, row count input, delete and done buttons. Increment
and decrement update the display in-place to preserve open panels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: replace Alpha Vantage with Twelve Data — actual CORS fix for ASX stocks

Root cause of previous fix not working:
  Alpha Vantage does NOT send CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin is absent).
  Every browser fetch to alphavantage.co is silently blocked by the browser's
  CORS policy — the catch block swallows the error and returns null.
  This was verified with curl -I (no Access-Control-Allow-Origin header present).

Real fix — Twelve Data:
  Verified CORS headers: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * ✓
  Supports ASX stocks with .AX suffix natively (BHP.AX, ANZ.AX, WOW.AX) ✓
  Free tier: 800 calls/day (vs Alpha Vantage's 25) ✓
  API: https://api.twelvedata.com/price?symbol=BHP.AX&apikey=KEY

Changes:
- priceFetcher.js: replace fetchAlphaVantagePrice with fetchTwelveDataPrice,
  rename key helpers (loadTwelveDataKey/saveTwelveDataKey), update routing comment
- Settings/index.jsx: swap all Alpha Vantage UI for Twelve Data (label, link,
  state, handlers, source card description)
- Investments/index.jsx: fix stale "Yahoo Finance / CoinGecko" hint text,
  import loadTwelveDataKey, improve error message to guide user to correct key
  based on ASX vs US symbol detection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): create versioned releases instead of overwriting latest (#10)

* fix(ci): create versioned releases instead of overwriting latest

Each push now creates a new release tagged v<date>.<run_number>
(e.g. v2026.03.06.42) marked as --latest, replacing the single
force-updated 'latest' tag that silently overwrote assets on an
old release object.

Also:
- fetch-depth: 0 to detect which skills changed vs previous release tag
- Release notes include a "Changed Skills" section showing what was updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): address Copilot review feedback on release workflow

- Append run_attempt to VERSION_TAG so re-runs don't collide with an
  existing tag/release
- Sort prev_tag by --sort=-v:refname for reliable vYYYY.MM.DD.<n> ordering
- Use full push range (prev_tag or github.event.before) instead of HEAD~1
  so multi-commit pushes are fully captured in Changed Skills
- Filter changed_skills to only directories that produced a .skill file,
  preventing non-skill dirs from appearing in release notes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: model drift stop-loss (20c threshold)

Force taker exit when model prob drifts > threshold against position,
catching the case where model and market move down together at the same
rate (edge stays constant but position is underwater with no recovery).

Data shows >25c drift has 0% win rate, 20-25c only 9% — clear losers.
Default 25c code, overridden to 20c in config.yaml on VPS.

commit.message:
fix: use SymbolEqualityComparer for symbol comparisons (#997)

## Summary
- Replace `ImmutableArray<IMethodSymbol>.Contains()` with
`IsInstanceOf()` in 2 analyzers
- `Contains()` uses reference equality; `IsInstanceOf()` uses
`SymbolEqualityComparer.Default`
- Fixes false negatives in
`RaiseEventArgumentsShouldMatchEventSignatureAnalyzer` and
`EventSetupHandlerShouldMatchEventTypeAnalyzer`

Closes #973

## Changes
- `RaiseEventArgumentsShouldMatchEventSignatureAnalyzer.cs`: Replace
`knownSymbols.Mock1Raise.Contains(methodSymbol.OriginalDefinition)` with
`methodSymbol.IsInstanceOf(knownSymbols.Mock1Raise)`
- `EventSetupHandlerShouldMatchEventTypeAnalyzer.cs`: Replace
`knownSymbols.ItIsAny.Contains(methodSymbol)` with
`methodSymbol.IsInstanceOf(knownSymbols.ItIsAny)`

## Test plan
- [x] Full test suite passes (2801 tests)
- [x] Build succeeds with 0 warnings

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Improved the detection mechanism in event handler analyzers to more
accurately identify specific method invocations using refined
symbol-checking logic.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

Co-authored-by: Richard Murillo <rjmurillo@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix split complementary zone matching and wedge display (#14)

* Add Features (Epics) section to overview and update /plan command

Add a new Features (Epics) section to docs/overview.md that lists all
documented work items organized by epic in table format with checkbox,
name, type, and status columns. Update the /plan command to automatically
add new docs to this section when created.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(color): narrow split complementary matching to per-point zones

Replace the broad 120-180 degree hue distance range with distance
checks from each split complementary center point (hue+150 and
hue+210). Paints must be within 25 degrees of either point to match,
consistent with the complementary scheme tolerance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update split complementary matching rule description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: mark split-complementary-zone-matching as completed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(color): widen scheme wedges to match actual matching zones

Complementary and split complementary wedge spans were 22 degrees
(±11) while the matching logic uses ±25 degree tolerance. Widen
wedge spans to 50 degrees (±25) so the visual indicators accurately
show which paints will be highlighted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(color-analysis): correct wedge span to match scheme tolerance

The span property is a half-width (used as center ± span), so span=50
created ±50° = 100° total arcs. Changed to span=25 for ±25° = 50° total,
matching the actual isMatchingScheme tolerance (d > 155 for complementary,
hueDistance < 25 for split).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(color-analysis): correct split complementary zone matching and wedge display (v0.9.1)

- Replace broad 120-180° hue range with per-point distance checks for split complementary
- Fix wedge span to use correct half-width value (25) matching the ±25° tolerance
- Update color-scheme-modes doc with accurate matching rule description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive Delaware data: 52 towns, 3 counties, 870 businesses

commit.message:
fix: prevent cooldown/in-laps from eliminating all normal laps in filter

_filter_short_laps() used the max lap distance as reference. When a
cooldown lap had 1.48× normal distance (5427m vs ~3660m), the 0.8×
threshold became 4342m — eliminating all 8 valid laps. Fix: first remove
outlier-long laps (>1.3× median distance), then apply short-lap filter
on remaining normal laps. Verified against 30+ real sessions: zero
regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: GPU multigrid race condition investigation & two-pass plan

Investigated GPU V-cycle divergence (18x/cycle on H100, 11 AMR levels).
Root cause: 8-color GS + 6th-order stencil (radius 3) races on GPU.
Tried 2nd-order smoother (stalls at discretization floor) and under-relaxed
Jacobian (not provably safe). Correct fix: two-pass GPU smoothing
(compute deltas read-only, then apply). Plan documented in DEVLOG.

No code changes — all experimental modifications reverted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  fetch + checkout origin/master to prevent rollback and handle

fix: remove CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY from migration schema

pgx executes the entire SQL file as an implicit transaction, so
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY fails. Use regular CREATE INDEX instead,
which is fine during startup migration with no concurrent writes.

commit.message:
Fix theme not persisting across page navigation

Two bugs: (1) inline script read Zustand's raw localStorage key which is a
JSON object {state:{pref:...},version:0}, not a plain string — now parses it
correctly with JSON.parse(r).state.pref. (2) <html data-theme="tweetsip"> in
JSX caused React to reset data-theme to light on every re-render (navigation).
Removed the static prop so React never manages that attribute — inline script
and applyTheme() own it exclusively.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: poll for review comments on all agent-authored PRs globally (#628)

* feat: tiered governance architecture for council jurisdiction (#590)

Three-layer governance model that prevents councils from voting on changes
to the system that runs the council itself:

- Layer 0 (Constitutional): NO council jurisdiction — human-only commits.
  Covers council orchestration, permission broker, spending limits, schema,
  protected-paths, middleware guards.
- Layer 1 (Structural): 75% supermajority + human approval required.
  Covers package.json, migrations, MCP tools, provider configs, validation.
- Layer 2 (Operational): 50% majority, no human approval needed.
  Covers everything else (routes, types, UI, tests, docs).

Implementation:
- New governance module (server/councils/governance.ts) with path
  classification, impact assessment, vote evaluation, and automation checks
- Migration 066: governance_votes + governance_member_votes tables,
  vote_type/governance_tier columns on council_launches
- Layer 0 hard-block on council launch for governance votes targeting
  constitutional paths
- Work task validation: git diff scanned for governance tier violations
  before completion
- Council namespace added to permission broker
- 33 new tests covering tier classification, impact assessment, vote
  evaluation, automation enforcement, and all DB operations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: poll for review comments on all agent-authored PRs globally

The mention polling service previously only searched for PR review
comments within repos/orgs listed in polling configs. This missed
feedback on external PRs (e.g. MattFlower/cc-focus#1).

Now searches `is:pr is:open author:USERNAME` without repo qualifier
to catch review comments across all of GitHub. Results are cached
per username per poll cycle to avoid redundant API calls.

  for ON CONFLICT clause to work)

- Rename 066_governance_tiers.ts → 067_governance_tiers.ts (066 taken by dedup_state)
